# html

```go
import "html"
```

Package html provides functions for escaping and unescaping HTML text.

## Index

- [func EscapeString\(s string\) string](<#EscapeString>)
- [func UnescapeString\(s string\) string](<#UnescapeString>)

<a name="EscapeString"></a>

## func [EscapeString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/html/escape.go#L178>)

```go
func EscapeString(s string) string
```

EscapeString escapes special characters like "\<" to become "&lt;". It escapes only five such characters: \<, \>, &, ' and ". [UnescapeString](<#UnescapeString>)\(EscapeString\(s\)\) == s always holds, but the converse isn't always true.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"html"
)

func main() {
	const s = `"Fran & Freddie's Diner" <tasty@example.com>`
	fmt.Println(html.EscapeString(s))
}
```

#### Output

```go
&#34;Fran &amp; Freddie&#39;s Diner&#34; &lt;tasty@example.com&gt;
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="UnescapeString"></a>

## func [UnescapeString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/html/escape.go#L187>)

```go
func UnescapeString(s string) string
```

UnescapeString unescapes entities like "&lt;" to become "\<". It unescapes a larger range of entities than [EscapeString](<#EscapeString>) escapes. For example, "&aacute;" unescapes to "á", as does "&\#225;" and "&\#xE1;". UnescapeString\([EscapeString](<#EscapeString>)\(s\)\) == s always holds, but the converse isn't always true.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"html"
)

func main() {
	const s = `&quot;Fran &amp; Freddie&#39;s Diner&quot; &lt;tasty@example.com&gt;`
	fmt.Println(html.UnescapeString(s))
}
```

#### Output

```go
"Fran & Freddie's Diner" <tasty@example.com>
```

</p>
</details>
